dimension_config:
elements:
  band:
    doc: 'A filter that is not associated with a particular instrument.  An abstract
      filter can be used to relate similar physical filters, and is typically the
      filter associated with coadds.

      '
    keys:
    - length: 32
      name: name
      type: string
    storage:
      cls: lsst.daf.butler.registry.dimensions.caching.CachingDimensionRecordStorage
      nested:
        cls: lsst.daf.butler.registry.dimensions.query.QueryDimensionRecordStorage
        view_of: physical_filter
  day_obs:
    doc: 'A day and night of observations that rolls over during daylight hours. The
      identifier is an decimal integer-concatenated date, i.e. YYYYMMDD, with the
      exact rollover time observatory-dependent.

      '
    keys:
    - name: id
      type: int
    requires:
    - instrument
    storage:
      cls: lsst.daf.butler.registry.dimensions.table.TableDimensionRecordStorage
  detector:
    doc: 'A detector associated with a particular instrument (not an observation of
      that detector; that requires specifying an exposure or visit as well).

      '
    keys:
    - name: id
      type: int
    - length: 32
      name: full_name
      type: string
    metadata:
    - length: 32
      name: name_in_raft
      type: string
    - doc: 'A string name for a group of detectors with an instrument-dependent interpretation.

        '
      length: 32
      name: raft
      type: string
    - doc: 'Role of the detector; typically one of "SCIENCE", "WAVEFRONT", or "GUIDE",
        though instruments may define additional values.

        '
      length: 32
      name: purpose
      type: string
    requires:
    - instrument
    storage:
      cls: lsst.daf.butler.registry.dimensions.caching.CachingDimensionRecordStorage
      nested:
        cls: lsst.daf.butler.registry.dimensions.table.TableDimensionRecordStorage
  exposure:
    doc: 'An observation associated with a particular instrument.  All direct observations
      are identified with an exposure, but derived datasets that may be based on more
      than one exposure (e.g. multiple snaps) are typically identified with visits
      instead, even for instruments that don''t have multiple exposures per visit.  As
      a result, instruments that don''t have multiple exposures per visit will typically
      have visit entries that are essentially duplicates of their exposure entries.

      The exposure table contains metadata entries that are relevant for calibration
      exposures, and does not duplicate entries in visit that would be the same for
      all exposures within a visit with the exception of the exposure.group entry.

      '
    implies:
    - physical_filter
    - group
    - day_obs
    keys:
    - name: id
      type: int
    - length: 64
      name: obs_id
      type: string
    metadata:
    - doc: Duration of the exposure with shutter open (seconds).
      name: exposure_time
      type: float
    - doc: Duration of the exposure with shutter closed (seconds).
      name: dark_time
      type: float
    - doc: The observation type of this exposure (e.g. dark, bias, science).
      length: 16
      name: observation_type
      type: string
    - doc: 'The reason this observation was taken. (e.g. science, filter scan, unknown).

        '
      length: 68
      name: observation_reason
      type: string
    - doc: 'Counter for the observation within a larger sequence. Context of the sequence
        number is observatory specific. Can be a global counter or counter within
        day_obs.

        '
      name: seq_num
      type: int
    - doc: 'Earliest sequence number that might be related to this exposure.

        '
      name: seq_start
      type: int
    - doc: 'Oldest sequence number that might be related to this exposure.

        '
      name: seq_end
      type: int
    - doc: Object of interest for this observation or survey field name.
      length: 64
      name: target_name
      type: string
    - doc: 'Observing program (survey, proposal, engineering project) identifier.

        '
      length: 64
      name: science_program
      type: string
    - doc: 'Tracking ICRS Right Ascension of boresight in degrees. Can be NULL for
        observations that are not on sky.

        '
      name: tracking_ra
      type: float
    - doc: 'Tracking ICRS Declination of boresight in degrees. Can be NULL for observations
        that are not on sky.

        '
      name: tracking_dec
      type: float
    - doc: 'Angle of the instrument focal plane on the sky in degrees. Can be NULL
        for observations that are not on sky, or for observations where the sky angle
        changes during the observation.

        '
      name: sky_angle
      type: float
    - doc: 'Azimuth of the telescope at the start of the exposure in degrees. Can
        be NULL for observations that are not on sky, or for observations where the
        azimuth is not relevant.

        '
      name: azimuth
      type: float
    - doc: 'Angle in degrees from the zenith at the start of the exposure.

        '
      name: zenith_angle
      type: float
    - doc: 'True if this exposure has some content that was simulated. This can be
        if the data itself were simulated or if parts of the header came from simulated
        systems, such as observations in the lab that are recorded as on-sky.

        '
      name: has_simulated
      type: bool
    - doc: 'True if the detector could see the sky during this exposure. This can
        be used to definitively determine whether an observation was on sky, taking
        into account dome state as well as camera shutter.

        '
      name: can_see_sky
      type: bool
    requires:
    - instrument
    storage:
      cls: lsst.daf.butler.registry.dimensions.table.TableDimensionRecordStorage
  group:
    doc: 'A group of observations defined at the time they were observed by the acquisition
      system.

      '
    keys:
    - length: 64
      name: name
      type: string
    requires:
    - instrument
    storage:
      cls: lsst.daf.butler.registry.dimensions.table.TableDimensionRecordStorage
  instrument:
    doc: 'An entity that produces observations.  An instrument defines a set of physical_filters
      and detectors and a numbering system for the exposures and visits that represent
      observations with it.

      '
    governor: true
    keys:
    - length: 32
      name: name
      type: string
    metadata:
    - doc: 'Maximum value for the ''visit'' field for visits associated with this
        instrument (exclusive).

        '
      name: visit_max
      type: int
    - doc: 'The preferred visit system for this instrument.

        '
      name: visit_system
      type: int
    - doc: 'Maximum value for the ''exposure'' field for exposures associated with
        this instrument (exclusive).

        '
      name: exposure_max
      type: int
    - doc: 'Maximum value for the ''detector'' field for detectors associated with
        this instrument (exclusive).

        '
      name: detector_max
      type: int
    - doc: 'Full class name of the Instrument class associated with this instrument.

        '
      length: 64
      name: class_name
      type: string
    storage:
      cls: lsst.daf.butler.registry.dimensions.governor.BasicGovernorDimensionRecordStorage
  patch:
    doc: 'A rectangular region within a tract.

      '
    keys:
    - name: id
      type: int
    metadata:
    - doc: 'Which column this patch occupies in the tract''s grid of patches.

        '
      name: cell_x
      nullable: false
      type: int
    - doc: 'Which row this patch occupies in the tract''s grid of patches.

        '
      name: cell_y
      nullable: false
      type: int
    requires:
    - skymap
    - tract
    storage:
      cls: lsst.daf.butler.registry.dimensions.table.TableDimensionRecordStorage
  physical_filter:
    doc: 'A filter associated with a particular instrument.  physical_filters are
      used to identify datasets that can only be associated with a single observation.

      '
    implies:
    - band
    keys:
    - length: 32
      name: name
      type: string
    requires:
    - instrument
    storage:
      cls: lsst.daf.butler.registry.dimensions.caching.CachingDimensionRecordStorage
      nested:
        cls: lsst.daf.butler.registry.dimensions.table.TableDimensionRecordStorage
  skymap:
    doc: 'A set of tracts and patches that subdivide the sky into rectangular regions
      with simple projections and intentional overlaps.

      '
    governor: true
    keys:
    - length: 64
      name: name
      type: string
    - doc: 'A hash of the skymap''s parameters.

        '
      name: hash
      nbytes: 40
      type: hash
    metadata:
    - doc: 'Maximum ID for tracts in this skymap, exclusive.

        '
      name: tract_max
      type: int
    - doc: 'Number of patches in the x direction in each tract.

        '
      name: patch_nx_max
      type: int
    - doc: 'Number of patches in the y direction in each tract.

        '
      name: patch_ny_max
      type: int
    storage:
      cls: lsst.daf.butler.registry.dimensions.governor.BasicGovernorDimensionRecordStorage
  ssp_balanced_index:
    doc: 'Dummy index for parallelization of linkPurify and other tasks.

      '
    keys:
    - name: id
      type: int
    requires:
    - ssp_hypothesis_table
    storage:
      cls: lsst.daf.butler.registry.dimensions.table.TableDimensionRecordStorage
  ssp_hypothesis_bundle:
    doc: 'A subset of hypothesized r, r'', r'''' over which a single quanta of HelioLinC
      searches for new SSOs.

      '
    keys:
    - name: id
      type: int
    requires:
    - ssp_hypothesis_table
    storage:
      cls: lsst.daf.butler.registry.dimensions.table.TableDimensionRecordStorage
  ssp_hypothesis_table:
    doc: 'A partitioned set of hypothesized r, r'', r'''' over which the solar system
      pipelines (SSP) search in parallel for new solar system objects.

      '
    keys:
    - length: 64
      name: name
      type: string
    storage:
      cls: lsst.daf.butler.registry.dimensions.table.TableDimensionRecordStorage
  subfilter:
    doc: 'A mathematical division of an band. Subfilters are used to model wavelength-dependent
      effects such as differential chromatic refraction.

      '
    keys:
    - name: id
      type: int
    requires:
    - band
    storage:
      cls: lsst.daf.butler.registry.dimensions.caching.CachingDimensionRecordStorage
      nested:
        cls: lsst.daf.butler.registry.dimensions.table.TableDimensionRecordStorage
  tract:
    doc: 'A large rectangular region mapped to the sky with a single map projection,
      associated with a particular skymap.

      '
    keys:
    - name: id
      type: int
    requires:
    - skymap
    storage:
      cls: lsst.daf.butler.registry.dimensions.table.TableDimensionRecordStorage
  visit:
    doc: 'A sequence of observations processed together, comprised of one or more
      exposures from the same instrument with the same pointing and physical_filter.
      The visit table contains metadata that is both meaningful only for science exposures
      and the same for all exposures in a visit.

      '
    implies:
    - physical_filter
    - day_obs
    keys:
    - name: id
      type: int
    - length: 64
      name: name
      type: string
    metadata:
    - doc: 'The sequence number of the first exposure that is part of this visit.

        '
      name: seq_num
      type: int
    - doc: 'The total exposure time of the visit in seconds.  This should be equal
        to the sum of the exposure_time values for all constituent exposures (i.e.
        it should not include time between exposures).

        '
      name: exposure_time
      type: float
    - doc: Object of interest for this visit or survey field name.
      length: 64
      name: target_name
      type: string
    - doc: 'The reason this visit was taken. (e.g. science, filter scan, unknown,
        various).

        '
      length: 68
      name: observation_reason
      type: string
    - doc: Observing program (survey or proposal) identifier.
      length: 64
      name: science_program
      type: string
    - doc: 'Approximate azimuth of the telescope in degrees during the visit. Can
        only be approximate since it is continually changing during an observation
        and multiple exposures can be combined from a relatively long period.

        '
      name: azimuth
      type: float
    - doc: 'Approximate zenith angle in degrees during the visit. Can only be approximate
        since it is continuously changing during an observation and multiple exposures
        can be combined from a relatively long period.

        '
      name: zenith_angle
      type: float
    requires:
    - instrument
    storage:
      cls: lsst.daf.butler.registry.dimensions.table.TableDimensionRecordStorage
  visit_definition:
    always_join: true
    doc: 'A many-to-many join table that relates exposures to the visits they belong
      to.

      '
    populated_by: visit
    requires:
    - exposure
    - visit
    storage:
      cls: lsst.daf.butler.registry.dimensions.table.TableDimensionRecordStorage
  visit_detector_region:
    doc: 'A many-to-many join table that provides region information for visit-detector
      combinations.

      '
    populated_by: visit
    requires:
    - visit
    - detector
    storage:
      cls: lsst.daf.butler.registry.dimensions.table.TableDimensionRecordStorage
  visit_system:
    doc: 'A system of self-consistent visit definitions, within which each exposure
      should appear at most once.

      A visit may belong to multiple visit systems, if the logical definitions for
      those systems happen to result in the same set of exposures - the main (and
      probably only) example is when a single-snap visit is observed, for which both
      the "one-to-one" visit system and a "group by header metadata" visit system
      will define the same single-exposure visit.

      '
    keys:
    - name: id
      type: int
    - length: 32
      name: name
      type: string
    requires:
    - instrument
    storage:
      cls: lsst.daf.butler.registry.dimensions.caching.CachingDimensionRecordStorage
      nested:
        cls: lsst.daf.butler.registry.dimensions.table.TableDimensionRecordStorage
  visit_system_membership:
    always_join: true
    doc: 'A many-to-many join table that relates visits to the visit_systems they
      belong to.

      '
    populated_by: visit
    requires:
    - visit
    - visit_system
    storage:
      cls: lsst.daf.butler.registry.dimensions.table.TableDimensionRecordStorage
namespace: daf_butler
packers:
  exposure_detector:
    cls: lsst.daf.butler.instrument.ObservationDimensionPacker
    dimensions:
    - instrument
    - exposure
    - detector
    fixed:
    - instrument
  tract_patch:
    cls: lsst.skymap.packers.SkyMapDimensionPacker
    dimensions:
    - skymap
    - tract
    - patch
    fixed:
    - skymap
  tract_patch_band:
    cls: lsst.skymap.packers.SkyMapDimensionPacker
    dimensions:
    - skymap
    - tract
    - patch
    - band
    fixed:
    - skymap
  visit_detector:
    cls: lsst.daf.butler.instrument.ObservationDimensionPacker
    dimensions:
    - instrument
    - visit
    - detector
    fixed:
    - instrument
skypix:
  common: htm7
  healpix:
    class: lsst.sphgeom.HealpixPixelization
    max_level: 17
  htm:
    class: lsst.sphgeom.HtmPixelization
    max_level: 24
topology:
  spatial:
    observation_regions:
    - visit_detector_region
    - visit
    skymap_regions:
    - patch
    - tract
  temporal:
    observation_timespans:
    - exposure
    - visit
    - day_obs
version: 8
